append_application_reply_to_history_as_n: Combine the response from the app with the chat history to output a new context.
auth_tmb_id_tip:
If enabled, a knowledge base will not be used if the user does not have access permissions to it when the app is published.
If disabled, the system will search from the selected knowledge bases without permission-based filtering.
can_not_loop: The node does not support circular nesting.
click_to_change_reference: Click to switch to input mode
click_to_change_value: Click to switch to variable reference mode
code.Reset template confirm: Are you sure you want to restore the code template? All inputs and outputs will be reset to the default values. Please save the current code first.
code.Switch language confirm: Changing the language will reset the code. Would you like to proceed?
code_sandbox_intro: Executing a script code in the sandbox can be used to perform complex data processing, but the syntax and available dependencies will be limited.
confirm_delete_field_tip: Are you sure you want to delete this field?
create_link_error: Error occurred while creating the link.
dataset_quote_role_tip:
System: Knowledge base references will be included in system messages for coherence, but constraints may be less effective. Additional debugging may be needed.
User: Knowledge base references will be included in user messages, and the position of the {{question}} variable must be specified. This may slightly affect coherence, but constraints can be enforced more effectively.
dynamic_input_description: Output values from the upstream nodes are received as variables, which can be used in LAF request parameters.
execute_different_branches_based_on_cond: Executes a branch based on the specified conditions.
extraction_requirements_description_deta:
Provide relevant context or prompts to guide extraction. 
Global variables are supported.
extraction_requirements_placeholder:
Example: 1. Current time: {{cTime}} You are a lab booking assistant and need to extract lab booking information from the text.
2. You are a Google search assistant and need to extract suitable keywords for Google searches from the text.
field_description_placeholder: Describe the purpose of the variable. If it is used for tool call, the description will affect the output quality of the model.
field_used_as_tool_input: Used as a tool call parameter
filter_description:
Tag filtering helps locate the exact files, improving retrieval accuracy and efficiency.
Example: When a user asks "How long is the warranty?", and it's determined the question is about Product A, tag filtering can narrow down results to Product A's files, avoiding irrelevant files that also mention "warranty".
form_input_result_tip: An object containing the full result
full_field_extraction_description: Returns true if all fields are filled in (either from model extraction or default values)
greater_than_or_equal_to: is greater than or equal to
http_body_placeholder: Use a syntax similar to APIFox. Enter a slash (/) to select a variable. Only string variables must be enclosed in double quotation marks. Ensure the string is in JSON format.
http_extract_output_description: Extract specific fields from the response using the JSONPath syntax.
http_raw_response_description: Raw HTTP response. The response data must be a string or in JSON format.
input_variable_list: Enter a slash (/) to select a variable.
intro_assigned_reply: This module supports replies with a specified message, often used as guidance or prompts. Non-string content is automatically converted to a string.
intro_custom_feedback: When triggered, this module adds feedback to the current conversation, useful for automatically tracking response quality.
intro_custom_plugin_output: Customize external output. When a tool is used, only the custom output is exposed.
intro_http_request: Send an HTTP request to perform a more complex action (such as online search or database query).
intro_knowledge_base_search_merge: Merge search results from multiple knowledge bases and sort them using the RRF method to provide output.
intro_laf_function_call: Call cloud functions under the LAF account.
intro_loop: Input an array and iterate over each element to execute the workflow.
intro_plugin_input: Configure inputs required for running a tool.
intro_question_classification:
Identify the question type based on the current question and the user's chat history. You can add multiple question types. Example:
Type 1: Greeting
Type 2: Product usage
Type 3: Product purchase
Type 4: Other
intro_question_optimization: Use the question optimization feature to increase the accuracy of knowledge base searches during continuous chats. The model will generate one or multiple new keywords based on the context to optimize the search results. This feature is built into the knowledge base search module and can also be used for a single search.
intro_text_concatenation: Processes fixed or input text to provide an output. Non-string data will be automatically converted into strings.
intro_text_content_extraction: Extract specific data from text, such as SQL statements, search keywords, or code.
intro_tool_call_termination: This module works with tool calling. When this module is executed, the tool call is terminated, and no replies are provided based on the tool call result.
intro_tool_params_config: This module works with tool calling. Customize tool call parameters and pass them to downstream nodes.
length_greater_than_or_equal_to: whose length is not shorter than
length_less_than_or_equal_to: whose length is not longer than
less_than_or_equal_to: is smaller than or equal to
intro_parallel_run: Input an array and run the workflow for each element in parallel, then aggregate the results.
parallel_run_end_intro: Select a variable as the output of the parallel run
parallel_run_max_concurrency_tip: Maximum number of tasks running in parallel (range: 1 to the upper limit, default 5).
parallel_run_max_retry_times_tip: Maximum number of retries for a failed task (range: 0–5, default 3). Set to 0 to disable retries.
can_not_parallel: This node cannot be used inside a Parallel Run node
parallel_success_results_desc: Outputs of all successful tasks, in input order (successful items only).
parallel_full_results_desc: Array of the same length as the input, each item is {success, message, data}: on success success=true, message is empty, data is the output; on failure success=false, message contains the error, data is null.
parallel_status_desc: Overall run status: success, partial_success, or failed.
parallel_task_not_reach_end: Sub-workflow did not reach the end node
parallel_task_interactive_not_supported: Sub-workflow triggered an interactive response, which is not supported in parallel runs
mouse_priority:
Mouse operations
- Click and hold to drag the canvas.
- Press Shift and click to select multiple objects.
optional_value_type_tip: You can specify one or multiple data types. When users add fields, they can only choose from these data types.
pan_priority:
Touchpad operations
- Tap to select multiple objects.
- Tap with two fingers to drag the canvas.
pass_returned_object_as_output_to_next_n: Use the return value as the output and pass it to the next node. The variable name must match the key in the return value.
plugin.Instruction_Tip: You can add a description to explain the purpose of the tool. The description will be displayed each time before the tool is used. Standard Markdown syntax is supported.
plugin_output_tool: Whether the field is used as the output when the tool runs as a tool.
quote_cite_tip: If you prevent prompt words from generating citation IDs, you will not be able to identify and display cited documents during conversations.
quote_content_placeholder:
Customize the structure of the referenced content to fit different scenarios. Use variables to configure the template.
{{q}} - Main content
{{a}} - Extra data
{{source}} - Source name
{{sourceId}} - Source ID
{{index}} - Sequence number of reference
quote_content_tip:
Customize the structure of the referenced content to fit different scenarios. Use variables to configure the template.
{{id}} - Unique reference ID
{{q}} - Main content
{{a}} - Extra data
{{source}} - Source name
{{sourceId}} - Source ID
{{index}} - Sequence number of reference
These variables are all optional. The default values are as follows: {{default}}
{{default}}
select_another_application_to_call: You can select another app to call.
special_array_format: Special array format. An empty array is returned when no results are found.
target_fields_description: A target field consists of the description and key. Multiple target fields can be extracted.
template.agent_intro: The model decides which tool to call.
template.agent_module_intro: Associate required tools and Skills to enable AI-driven autonomous invocation and planning
agent.prompt_skill_tip: @ to select Skills and tools, "/" to select variables
template.dataset_search_intro: Retrieves relevant reference materials from the knowledge base using standard retrieval or multi-round intelligent retrieval.
template.forbid_stream_desc: Force nested apps to run in non-streaming mode.
these_variables_will_be_input_parameters: These variables will be used as input parameters for code execution.
tool_params.enum_values_tip: Valid values for the field. One value per line.
trigger_after_application_completion: It will be triggered after the app is fully executed.
update_link_error: Error occurred while updating the link.
update_specified_node_output_or_global_v: Update output for a specified node or update global variables.
variable_picker_tips: Enter a node name or variable name.
workflow.exit_tips: Your changes are not saved and will be discarded if you exit directly.
Confirm_sync_node:
Nodes will be updated to the latest configuration. Fields that do not exist in the template (including all custom fields) will be deleted.
If the fields are complex, it is recommended to copy the node before updating it.
error_info_returns_empty_on_success: Error message returned when code execution error occurs. If the code is executed successfully, a null value is returned.
execute_a_simple_script_code_usually_for: Runs a short script, typically for complex data processing.
plugin_file_abandon_tip:
Plugin global file upload has been deprecated, please adjust it as soon as possible. 
Related functions can be achieved through plug-in input and adding image type input.
support_code_language: Libraries that can be imported in the code: pandas, numpy
customer_service.fallback_reply_default: Sorry, I couldn't find an answer. You can try rephrasing your question, and I'll also pass your feedback to the relevant team.
customer_service.answer_mode_desc: How to respond when knowledge base has relevant content
customer_service.welcome_text_default: Hello! How can I help you?
tag_filter_no_tags: No tags available in the selected dataset, please create tags in the dataset first
tag_filter_auto_tip: Auto filter (auto-generate filter conditions based on user query, coming soon)
tag_filter_label_tooltip:
Tag filtering helps pinpoint the right files, improving retrieval accuracy and efficiency.
E.g., if a user asks "How long is the validity period?" and it's determined they're asking about Product A, tag filtering can narrow the search to Product A files, avoiding results from other files that also mention "validity period".
